description: Curated shortlist of exemplary, battle-tested Waterfall EFFECTTEMPLATEs to offer as a starting point when a user creates a new plume in EngineEditor. Selected from the installed GameData by usageCount (how many installed engine .cfgs reference the template) and manual review of layering/controller/color quality. All templateName values verified to resolve via GET /api/template in this install. RealPlume is out of scope.

templates.whyGood: Reference 13-effect kerolox skeleton (core/mid/vac triple-layer body, 3-segment shock stack, wisp, bone-rigged inner glow, distortion, light) with textbook throttle+atmosphereDepth curve shaping.
Same author lineage as waterfall-kerolox-lower-4, tuned for vacuum operation (longer plume, no shock stack, higher usageCount in this install).
Minimal 2-effect kerolox plume (flame + bone-rigged Core) - good lightweight starter for engines that don't need the full multi-layer treatment.

Highest-usage hydrolox template in the install; demonstrates the near-invisible-plume convention (low brightness, high _Fresnel rim glow) plus a dedicated shock-diamond mesh (fx-simple-shock-1) and Cherenkov-style engine light color curve.
Vacuum-tuned hydrolox counterpart; wide/faint plume2 layers with high _Fresnel (up to 7) to keep the plume readable when almost fully additive-transparent.

Full multi-layer methalox plume with the characteristic purple/violet outer-plume tint over a white-hot orange core; includes throttle/end-flame/distort/light layers matching the kerolox skeleton for easy comparison.
Highest-usage methalox template; vacuum-tuned violet/magenta plume with paired shock and inner-glow layers.
Most-used hypergolic template in the install by a wide margin; pink-core-to-pale-blue-envelope look plus a distinct violet coreBlue accent layer characteristic of NTO/UDMH propellants.
Second-most-used hypergolic template; deeper orange-red tint variant for storable-oxidizer engines, good alternate starter alongside the white-upper template.

By far the single most-referenced template in this entire install; full ignition-flash (alpha-animated core), tan-smoke main plume, and a complete 6-effect burnout/sparkout sequence (sparkout, sparkout2, trickleout, Fireball, smokeout, smokeout2) - the reference solid-motor starter.
Second-most-used solid template overall; simpler 5-effect structure with distinct core/plumeOuter/nozzleExit/lowThrust layers, white-to-orange gradient tuned for vacuum/upper-stage solids.

Clean, minimal ion-thruster template (single griddedPlume1 effect + light) with the saturated blue-violet signature typical of gridded ion engines; good compact starter to layer additional detail onto.

Demonstrates the concentric-ring ion-plume technique (centerSource/sourceFlame/coreFlame/ringSource/ringFlame/ringInside) rather than a single cone - a distinct layering pattern worth offering alongside the simpler gridded-xenon template.

Reference NTR template: hydrolox-adjacent pale plume plus a distinct pink/magenta 'plume-core' and rimglow layer that reads as the reactor-heated core - the diagnostic NTR look absent from chemical-engine templates.

Adds a dedicated cherenkov glow effect (blue-violet, low-alpha, additive) on top of the standard NTR plume/shock layers - worth using whenever a mission wants the 'reactor glow' look explicitly called out.

Minimal single-effect propellant-agnostic hot-metal nozzle ring, meant to be layered onto any plume template rather than used standalone; orange variant of a 4-color family (orange/blue/white/yellow) that share one mesh/scale/curve pattern.
